  Section 3 — New Features In Detail  
 
Change 01 — Med-Gemma for Medical Analysis  
Why: Generic AI models are not trained on medical data. Med -Gemma is Google's open 
medical AI model — trained specifically on clinical text, radiology reports, and pathology. 
Judges who know healthcare will immediately respect this over a general -purpose m odel.  
 
What Changes  
• Replace Claude analysis in analyze -report edge function with Med -Gemma API  
• Med-Gemma outputs ICD -10 codes natively — required for insurance claims  
• Clinical accuracy is higher for rare conditions and drug interactions  
• Pitch narrative: 'We use the same AI model Google built for clinicians'  
 
Integration  
// Replace in analyze -report edge function  
const response = await 
fetch('https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models/medgemma -4b-
it:generateContent', {  
  method: 'POST',  
  headers: { 'Authorization': `Bearer ${GEMINI_API_KEY}` },  
  body: JSON.stringify({  
    contents: [{ parts: [{ text: medicalPrompt }] }]  
  }) 
}) 
 
Change 02 — FHIR Universal Report Standardisation  
Why: Apollo calls it 'Glycated Haemoglobin'. Fortis calls it 'HbA1c (HPLC Method)'. Government 
hospitals call it 'GHB'. Three labs. Same test. Three formats. No system can compare them. 
FHIR is the global standard used by WHO, NHS, and India's Ayushman Bha rat Digital Mission.  
 
What FHIR Standardisation Does  
• Every report converted to FHIR DiagnosticReport format on upload  
• Every metric gets a LOINC code — universal medical test identifier  
• Units standardised to UCUM format — no more % vs mmol/L confusion  
• Reference ranges normalised — HIGH/LOW/NORMAL consistently applied  
• Enables fraud detection — all values now directly comparable  
• Enables insurance claims — ICD-10 codes extracted automatically  
 FHIR Output Example  
{ 
  "resourceType": "DiagnosticReport",  
  "result": [{  
    "code": {  
      "coding": [{ "system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "4548 -4", "display": 
"HbA1c" }]  
    }, 
    "valueQuantity": { "value": 8.2, "unit": "%" },  
    "interpretation": "HIGH"  
  }], 
  "medichainMetadata": {  
    "riskScore": 85,  
    "fraudScore": 2,  
    "ipfsCid": "QmX7b3Fk9...",  
    "originalLabName": "Apollo Diagnostics"  
  } 
} 
 
Change 03 — Fraud Detection Engine  
Why: Insurance fraud costs India ₹45,000 crore per year. The primary mechanism is falsified lab 
reports — values changed to justify expensive treatments or inflate claims. Because MediChain 
stores every report in FHIR format with all key values, physiologi cally impossible changes can 
be detected automatically.  
 
Fraud Detection Rules  
• HbA1c cannot change more than 1% per month — flag if it does  
• Same lab + same date + different values = duplicate tampering  
• Risk score 50% above patient baseline = anomaly flag  
• Cholesterol cannot change more than 10% per week naturally  
• Blood pressure cannot change more than 20 mmHg per day naturally  
• Each flag contributes to a fraud score 0 -100 
 
Output  
• FRAUD SCORE shown on every report (0 = clean, 100 = critical)  
• Specific flags shown to doctor and insurance company  
• Blockchain stores fraud score — tamper -proof audit trail  
• Insurance company can verify fraud score independently via IPFS CID  
 
Change 04 — Three -Document PDF Generation  
Why: Every doctor visit currently requires three documents — clinical notes, insurance claim, 
health record — all done separately, by hand, by different people. This is why claims get rejected, doctors burn out, and fraud proliferates. MediChain generates all three from one AI 
conversation.  
 
Document 1 — Clinical Notes PDF  
Input: Doctor speaks rough post -visit notes (voice or text)  
Output: Structured clinical documentation with:  
• Chief complaint, history of present illness  
• Physical examination findings  
• Diagnosis with ICD -10 code  
• Treatment plan and medications  
• Follow -up instructions  
 
Document 2 — Insurance Claim PDF  
Input: FHIR report + clinical notes + doctor ID  
Output: Ready -to-submit insurance claim with:  
• ICD-10 diagnosis codes (extracted by Med -Gemma)  
• CPT procedure codes  
• IPFS CID as tamper -proof evidence attachment  
• Blockchain transaction hash for verification  
• Fraud score certificate  
 
Document 3 — Health Record Update PDF  
Input: All previous records + new visit data  
Output: Updated longitudinal health record with:  
• Complete condition history  
• Medication timeline  
• Risk score trend chart  
• All previous IPFS CIDs for full auditability  
 
Change 05 — Doctor WhatsApp Reminder  
When a patient grants a doctor access to their record, the doctor immediately receives a 
WhatsApp message. No polling. No dashboard check. Instant notification.  
 
Message Format  
Dr. Priya — your patient has shared a medical record with you.  
 
Risk Score: 85/100 HIGH  
Conditions: Diabetes, Hypertension  Specialist Match: Endocrinologist  
 
View full records: medichain.app/doctor  
Access expires: 48 hours  
 
Powered by MediChain AI  
 
Trigger  
• Patient clicks 'Grant Access' on web app  
• Smart contract grantTimedAccess() fires  
• Backend listens for on -chain event  
• Twilio sends WhatsApp to doctor's registered number  
• Doctor clicks link → Privy login → sees full records  
 
Change 06 — Privy MPC Wallets For Web App  
Why: Custodial wallets where the backend holds all keys are not genuinely decentralised. Privy 
uses MPC — Multi -Party Computation — to split the key mathematically between Privy's 
servers and the user's device. Neither side alone can sign a transaction.  
 
What MPC Means In Plain English  
Think of it like a bank vault that needs two keys to open. Privy has one key. The user's device 
has the other. Neither works alone. Even if Privy's entire server is compromised, the attacker 
gets cryptographically useless data.  
 
User Experience  
• User clicks 'Continue with Google' — exactly like any web app  
• Privy creates real MPC wallet invisibly — user never sees it  
• All blockchain transactions signed with user's actual wallet  
• User can export private key to MetaMask anytime for full self -custody  
• Zero crypto knowledge required  
 
Architecture Decision  
• Web App: Privy MPC wallets — genuinely trustless  
• WhatsApp: AI diagnostics only — no blockchain  
• Bridge: medichain.app/claim — WhatsApp users graduate to Web3  
 
  Section 4 — Final Architecture  
 
Two-Tier Design  
 
WhatsApp — Tier 1  Web App — Tier 2  
For 2 billion mainstream users  
AI diagnostics — no blockchain  
Med-Gemma medical analysis  
Risk score + conditions + specialist  
Doctor WhatsApp notification  
Link to web app for full sovereignty  For users who want sovereignty  
Privy MPC wallet via Google login  
IPFS + AES -256 encryption  
Timed on -chain access control  
On-chain audit trail  
PDF generation (3 documents)  
Full FHIR report standardisation  
Fraud detection engine  
 
The Bridge — Claim Your Wallet  
This is the most important architectural feature. It is the physical manifestation of Track 6's 
objective — bridging Web2 habits with Web3 infrastructure.  
 
• WhatsApp user receives AI analysis — pure Web2 experience  
• After 2 -3 reports, MediChain sends: 'Want your records secured forever? Visit 
medichain.app/claim'  
• User visits web app, logs in with Google via Privy  
• Privy creates MPC wallet — user now has real on -chain identity  
• All WhatsApp reports imported and secured on IPFS + blockchain  
• User is now fully self -sovereign — can export key to MetaMask anytime  
 
  Section 5 — Pitch & Judge Q&A  
 
The 60 -Second Opening  
My sister is a doctor. She sees 80 patients a day and spends 3 hours on 
paperwork. Every visit creates three documents — clinical notes, 
insurance claims, health records — all done separately, by hand, from 
memory. That's why claims get rejected. That's wh y doctors burn out. 
That's why insurance fraud costs India ₹45,000 crore a year.  
MediChain fixes this. Patient uploads their report. Our AI — Med-Gemma, 
the same model Google built for clinicians — analyzes it, generates a risk 
score, detects conditions, and flags potential fraud by comparing values 
against the patient's entire history  secured on blockchain.  
Doctor speaks rough notes after the visit. We generate three 
documents in two minutes. Clinical note. Insurance claim. Health 
record. All verified on blockchain. All tamper -proof. And the entire 
experience works on WhatsApp — no app, no wallet, no crypto. Two 
billion users. Zero friction.  
 
Judge Q&A — Prepared Answers  
 
Q: Isn't this just centralised with a blockchain sticker?  
Every report is stored on IPFS — permanently, decentrally, uncensorable. Web app users have 
Privy MPC wallets — neither us nor Privy holds the full key. The trust model is identical to 
Coinbase's consumer wallet. WhatsApp users use custodial wallets — same  as any Web3 
onboarding product — with a clear path to full self -custody via our claim -your-wallet flow.  
 
Q: Why Med -Gemma instead of ChatGPT?  
Med-Gemma is Google's open medical AI model trained specifically on clinical text, pathology 
reports, and radiology. It outputs ICD -10 codes natively — required for insurance claims. 
General AI models hallucinate medical details. Med -Gemma is clinically va lidated.  
 
Q: What makes your fraud detection reliable?  
We use physiological impossibility rules — not pattern matching. HbA1c physically cannot 
change more than 1% per month. Blood pressure cannot jump 60 mmHg in 24 hours naturally. 
These are biological constants. If a report shows otherwise, it was tampered w ith. The rules are 
based on published clinical research, not heuristics.  
 Q: Why WhatsApp instead of an app?  
India has 530 million WhatsApp users including rural populations who will never download a 
medical app. Our target patients — chronic disease patients managing diabetes, hypertension, 
cardiac conditions — are often elderly. WhatsApp is what they already us e. We meet users 
where they are.  
 
Q: What happens if your servers go down?  
Reports live on IPFS independently of our infrastructure. The blockchain stores content 
addresses. Patients can access their data directly from IPFS with their CID. We are not a single 
point of failure. Web app users with Privy MPC wallets retain full acce ss to their wallet and data 
regardless of MediChain's operational status.

Total estimated remaining build time: ~22 hours  
Critical non -negotiable: Demo rehearsal. A working demo that hesitates loses to a simpler demo 
that flows perfectly.  
 
MediChain AI — Decentralised. Intelligent. Patient -first.

Change 02 — FHIR Universal Report Standardisation  
Why: Apollo calls it 'Glycated Haemoglobin'. Fortis calls it 'HbA1c (HPLC Method)'. Government

What FHIR Standardisation Does

• Every metric gets a LOINC code — universal medical test identifier  
• Units standardised to UCUM format — no more % vs mmol/L confusion  
• Reference ranges normalised — HIGH/LOW/NORMAL consistently applied  
• Enables fraud detection — all values now directly comparable  
• Enables insurance claims — ICD-10 codes extracted automatically

Change 03 — Fraud Detection Engine  
Why: Insurance fraud costs India ₹45,000 crore per year. The primary mechanism is falsified lab 
reports — values changed to justify expensive treatments or inflate claims. Because MediChain 
stores every report in FHIR format with all key values, physiologi cally impossible changes can 
be detected automatically.  
 
Fraud Detection Rules  
• HbA1c cannot change more than 1% per month — flag if it does  
• Same lab + same date + different values = duplicate tampering  
• Risk score 50% above patient baseline = anomaly flag  
• Cholesterol cannot change more than 10% per week naturally  
• Blood pressure cannot change more than 20 mmHg per day naturally  
• Each flag contributes to a fraud score 0 -100 
 
Output  
• FRAUD SCORE shown on every report (0 = clean, 100 = critical)  
• Specific flags shown to doctor and insurance company  
• Blockchain stores fraud score — tamper -proof audit trail  
• Insurance company can verify fraud score independently via IPFS CID

Change 06 — Privy MPC Wallets For Web App  
Why: Custodial wallets where the backend holds all keys are not genuinely decentralised. Privy 
uses MPC — Multi -Party Computation — to split the key mathematically between Privy's 
servers and the user's device. Neither side alone can sign a transaction.

Think of it like a bank vault that needs two keys to open. Privy has one key. The user's device 
has the other. Neither works alone. Even if Privy's entire server is compromised, the attacker 
gets cryptographically useless data.

• Privy creates real MPC wallet invisibly — user never sees it  
• All blockchain transactions signed with user's actual wallet  
• User can export private key to MetaMask anytime for full self -custody  
• Zero crypto knowledge required  
 
Architecture Decision  
• Web App: Privy MPC wallets — genuinely trustless  
• WhatsApp: AI diagnostics only — no blockchain  
• Bridge: medichain.app/claim — WhatsApp users graduate to Web3

Change 02 — FHIR Universal Report Standardisation  
Why: Apollo calls it 'Glycated Haemoglobin'. Fortis calls it 'HbA1c (HPLC Method)'. Government 
hospitals call it 'GHB'. Three labs. Same test. Three formats. No system can compare them. 
FHIR is the global standard used by WHO, NHS, and India's Ayushman Bha rat Digital Mission.  
 
What FHIR Standardisation Does  
• Every report converted to FHIR DiagnosticReport format on upload  
• Every metric gets a LOINC code — universal medical test identifier  
• Units standardised to UCUM format — no more % vs mmol/L confusion  
• Reference ranges normalised — HIGH/LOW/NORMAL consistently applied  
• Enables fraud detection — all values now directly comparable  
• Enables insurance claims — ICD-10 codes extracted automatically

be detected automatically.

• Blood pressure cannot change more than 20 mmHg per day naturally  
• Each flag contributes to a fraud score 0 -100

Change 02 — FHIR Universal Report Standardisation  
Why: Apollo calls it 'Glycated Haemoglobin'. Fortis calls it 'HbA1c (HPLC Method)'. Government 
hospitals call it 'GHB'. Three labs. Same test. Three formats. No system can compare them. 
FHIR is the global standard used by WHO, NHS, and India's Ayushman Bha rat Digital Mission.  
 
What FHIR Standardisation Does  
• Every report converted to FHIR DiagnosticReport format on upload  
• Every metric gets a LOINC code — universal medical test identifier  
• Units standardised to UCUM format — no more % vs mmol/L confusion  
• Reference ranges normalised — HIGH/LOW/NORMAL consistently applied  
• Enables fraud detection — all values now directly comparable  
• Enables insurance claims — ICD-10 codes extracted automatically

Change 03 — Fraud Detection Engine  
Why: Insurance fraud costs India ₹45,000 crore per year. The primary mechanism is falsified lab 
reports — values changed to justify expensive treatments or inflate claims. Because MediChain 
stores every report in FHIR format with all key values, physiologi cally impossible changes can 
be detected automatically.  
 
Fraud Detection Rules  
• HbA1c cannot change more than 1% per month — flag if it does  
• Same lab + same date + different values = duplicate tampering  
• Risk score 50% above patient baseline = anomaly flag  
• Cholesterol cannot change more than 10% per week naturally  
• Blood pressure cannot change more than 20 mmHg per day naturally  
• Each flag contributes to a fraud score 0 -100 
 
Output  
• FRAUD SCORE shown on every report (0 = clean, 100 = critical)  
• Specific flags shown to doctor and insurance company  
• Blockchain stores fraud score — tamper -proof audit trail  
• Insurance company can verify fraud score independently via IPFS CID

Change 06 — Privy MPC Wallets For Web App  
Why: Custodial wallets where the backend holds all keys are not genuinely decentralised. Privy 
uses MPC — Multi -Party Computation — to split the key mathematically between Privy's 
servers and the user's device. Neither side alone can sign a transaction.  
 
What MPC Means In Plain English  
Think of it like a bank vault that needs two keys to open. Privy has one key. The user's device 
has the other. Neither works alone. Even if Privy's entire server is compromised, the attacker 
gets cryptographically useless data.  
 
User Experience  
• User clicks 'Continue with Google' — exactly like any web app  
• Privy creates real MPC wallet invisibly — user never sees it  
• All blockchain transactions signed with user's actual wallet  
• User can export private key to MetaMask anytime for full self -custody  
• Zero crypto knowledge required  
 
Architecture Decision  
• Web App: Privy MPC wallets — genuinely trustless  
• WhatsApp: AI diagnostics only — no blockchain  
• Bridge: medichain.app/claim — WhatsApp users graduate to Web3

Judge Q&A — Prepared Answers  
 
Q: Isn't this just centralised with a blockchain sticker?  
Every report is stored on IPFS — permanently, decentrally, uncensorable. Web app users have 
Privy MPC wallets — neither us nor Privy holds the full key. The trust model is identical to 
Coinbase's consumer wallet. WhatsApp users use custodial wallets — same  as any Web3 
onboarding product — with a clear path to full self -custody via our claim -your-wallet flow.  
 
Q: Why Med -Gemma instead of ChatGPT?  
Med-Gemma is Google's open medical AI model trained specifically on clinical text, pathology 
reports, and radiology. It outputs ICD -10 codes natively — required for insurance claims. 
General AI models hallucinate medical details. Med -Gemma is clinically va lidated.  
 
Q: What makes your fraud detection reliable?  
We use physiological impossibility rules — not pattern matching. HbA1c physically cannot 
change more than 1% per month. Blood pressure cannot jump 60 mmHg in 24 hours naturally. 
These are biological constants. If a report shows otherwise, it was tampered w ith. The rules are 
based on published clinical research, not heuristics.  
 Q: Why WhatsApp instead of an app?  
India has 530 million WhatsApp users including rural populations who will never download a 
medical app. Our target patients — chronic disease patients managing diabetes, hypertension, 
cardiac conditions — are often elderly. WhatsApp is what they already us e. We meet users 
where they are.  
 
Q: What happens if your servers go down?  
Reports live on IPFS independently of our infrastructure. The blockchain stores content 
addresses. Patients can access their data directly from IPFS with their CID. We are not a single 
point of failure. Web app users with Privy MPC wallets retain full acce ss to their wallet and data 
regardless of MediChain's operational status.
